text.title: BackgroundService Code Examples and Antipatterns
text.summary: Queue consumer, timed cleanup, health check wiring, and DI registration examples; plus antipatterns to reject.

example.note: CreateScope() per item prevents captive DbContext. stoppingToken passed to ReadAllAsync exits the loop on shutdown.
PeriodicTimer fires on wall-clock schedule. WaitForNextTickAsync exits on cancellation.

antipattern.description: Task.Run(async () => { while (true) { ... await Task.Delay(5000); } }) in Program.cs instead of BackgroundService.
antipattern.reason: Bypasses IHost lifecycle, cancellation, DI scoping, and health check integration.
No back-pressure; producer can enqueue faster than the worker consumes, exhausting memory under load.

antipattern.description: DbContext injected into BackgroundService constructor (captive dependency).
antipattern.reason: Singleton BackgroundService holds a scoped DbContext for the process lifetime, breaking EF Core's connection management.

text.reference: Subagent prompt for generating the full BackgroundService + queue + DI + test
